title: thymus ciliatus
intro: Thymus ciliatus, also known as the red marjoram or wild marjoram, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Thymus ciliatus include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, and act as a natural antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Thymus ciliatus can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or essential oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
